paragraphs: This is Ruby Saltbush (Enchylaena tomentosa) sold under the Tucker Bush native-bushfood brand. Despite the shared common name, it belongs to a different genus from the Atriplex saltbushes. It is a small, sometimes straggly native shrub with greyish, hairy foliage and linear, succulent leaves, found across the semi-arid and arid areas of all mainland Australian states.

Its standout feature is the small berry-like fruit (around 5 to 8 mm), which ripens through yellow, orange and red and has a crisp, salty-sweet flavour with an edible seed inside. The berries can be eaten raw, and it is a hardy, frost and salt tolerant succulent suited to dry-climate gardens.

claims.text: Ruby Saltbush is Enchylaena tomentosa, a different genus from the Atriplex saltbushes, sold here under the Tucker Bush brand
Small, sometimes straggly shrub with greyish hairy foliage and linear succulent leaves
Native across semi-arid and arid areas of all mainland Australian states
Berry-like fruit about 5 to 8 mm ripens yellow to red with a salty-sweet flavour and edible seed
Berries can be eaten raw; plant is hardy, frost and salt tolerant

sources.name: Australian Native Plants Society: Enchylaena tomentosa
